THE COURT RULES ON THE PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE TO JUDGE WOOLARD PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 170.6 AS FOLLOWS: DENIED AS UNTIMELY, THE COURT ALSO HAS NO PREJUDICE AGAINST EITHER SIDE, AND, THE COURT ALSO NOTES FOR THE RECORD, THAT SGT. RON WOOLARD, HAS NO CONNECTION WITH THIS CASE, AND THE COURT DOES NOT KNOW A SGT. REYNOLDS. THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS TO THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION. THE MOTION IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 2, 2009. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WILL BE ALLOWED TO FILE A REPLY, NO LONGER THAN FIVE PAGES, WITH NO ATTACHMENTS, WHICH WILL BE DUE BY MARCH 26, 2009. THE MEDALLION WILL BE HELD IN TRUST BY THE TAXI COMMISSIONER, ONCE IT IS SURRENDERED. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5759

MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER SAYED RAHIMI Motion to Grant Petition for Writ of Administrative Mandamus and/or Mandate. Matter submitted as of this date. No appearances necessary. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Grant Petition For Writs Of Adminstrative Mandamus And/Or Mandate
Off Calendar May-26-2010 Continued To Jun-29-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Petitioner SAYED RAHIMI, Motion to Grant Petitioner for Writ of Admininistrative Mandamus and/or Mandate. Appearances: Geoffrey Rotwein, Esq. appearing for Petitioner and Moving party; Wayne Kessler Snodgrass, Esq. appearing for Respondents. Petitioner's counsel requests a statement of decision before argument heard. Hearing held and argued. The Court requests that counsel both exchange proposed statements to each other, and that each counsel is to comment on the other's proposed statement. Thereafter, the proposed statements with comments will be submitted to the Court no later than 6/29/2010 on a disk in a Word Document format. (Comments may be submitted in writing.) The Matter is continued to 6/29/2010, when it will be deemed as submitted to the Court for decision. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Petitioner Sayed Rahimi'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Ruling Of His Ex Parte Application For Order For Stay License And Card Suspension Pending Final Determination Of Writ Proceeding Filed Jan-30-2009 In Pro Per
Off Calendar Mar-04-2009 Continued To Apr-02-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Petitioner Sayed Rahimi's motion for reconsideration of ruling of his ex parte application for order for stay license and Card suspension pending final determination of Writ. The Court rules on the Peremptory Challenge to Judge Woolard pursuant to CCP section 170.6 as follows: denied as untimely, the Court also has no prejudice against either side, and, the Court also notes for the record, that Sgt. Ron Woolard, has no connection with this case, and the Court does not know a Sgt. Reynolds. The Court vacates its tentative ruling as to the Motion for reconsideration. The motion is continued to April 2, 2009. Plaintiff's counsel will be allowed to file a reply, no longer than five pages, with no attachments, which will be due by March 26, 2009. The Medallion will be held in trust by the Taxi Commissioner, once it is surrendered. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
